ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Drumstick Braised Pork Rice Set; closest match is braised pork rice food product. The set includes a beverage and side dish not captured in the ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pork, cooked — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for the braised pork component of this composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a composite code for braised pork rice set meals. A0CKW (cooked pork) is used as the primary protein anchor. The rice and side components are not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite Singaporean hawker dish.

serving.description: 1 set (rice + braised pork drumstick + 1 side + 1 large beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Drumstick Braised Pork Rice Set weighs approximately 550 g and includes a portion of steamed white rice (approximately 200 g), one braised pork drumstick (approximately 200 g), one side dish such as a braised egg or tofu (approximately 80 g), and a large beverage (approximately 400 ml, not included in solid weight).

nutrient_source.title: Drumstick Braised Pork Rice Set — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Drumstick Braised Pork Rice Set (cr_id G00010391). Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and has been estimated from USDA FoodData Central braised pork composite data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice, steamed, as analogous GI anchor for rice-dominant composite dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Drumstick Braised Pork Rice Set as a composite dish. GI of 72 is assigned based on the dominant carbohydrate component being steamed white rice (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 49.0) / 100 ≈ 35.3, based on estimated 195 g carbohydrate-contributing food in a 550 g serving yielding approximately 49 g available carbohydrate. The braising sauce contributes minor additional sugar (0.21 g/100g) which marginally elevates GL but does not change the GI category.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium from soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and oyster sauce in braising liquid
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced braising sauce or sauce on the side, as the composite sodium of 231 mg/100g underrepresents the true per-serving sodium load from soy-based braising liquids; a full set serving may deliver 900–1200 mg sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice dominates carbohydrate profile at 25.2 g/100g with GI of approximately 72
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider requesting brown rice or reducing the rice portion to lower glycaemic load, and should monitor total carbohydrate intake per full serving, as the braising sauce also contributes additional sugar to the glycaemic burden.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from pork and elevated sodium from soy-based braising sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and request reduced sauce to manage saturated fat and sodium intake; the pork drumstick contributes saturated fat that may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed frequently.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid from red meat (pork) and high sodium from braising sauce worsening tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting pork with chicken or tofu to reduce arachidonic acid intake, and should request reduced braising sauce to limit sodium-driven tissue inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Drumstick Braised Pork Rice Set is a popular Singaporean hawker and coffeeshop meal consisting of steamed white rice served with a braised pork drumstick (pork knuckle or hock), typically accompanied by one side dish such as a braised egg, tofu, or preserved vegetables, and one large beverage. The pork is slow-braised in a soy-based master stock containing soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, five-spice powder, and sugar, producing tender, deeply flavoured meat with a rich, savoury-sweet glaze. The dish is a Singaporean Chinese hawker staple with Hokkien and Teochew culinary roots, closely related to the Taiwanese lu rou fan tradition. Per 100 g, the composite dish provides 164 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat, and 25.19 g carbohydrate, with 3.7 g dietary fibre and 231 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is approximately 72 (high), driven by the steamed white rice base. Clinically, the dish presents high concern for individuals with hypertension due to the sodium-dense braising sauce, high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to the high-GI rice and added sugar in the sauce, and moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat from pork and elevated sodium. The set format with a large beverage means total caloric and sodium intake per meal occasion substantially exceeds the per-100g figures.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a drumstick braised pork rice set?
Is lu rou fan good for diabetics?
Sodium content of braised pork rice
Can I eat braised pork rice if I have high blood pressure?
What is in a braised pork rice set?
Is braised pork rice suitable for heart patients?

confidence_reason: Per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010391, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central braised pork composite data. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database using steamed white rice as the dominant carbohydrate anchor.
notes: Saturated fat (1.4 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central braised pork and rice composite data as HPB does not report saturated fat for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite Singaporean hawker dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is confirmed zero per the HPB source entry (confirmed zero). The set description states it includes 1 large beverage and 1 side; beverage nutritional contribution is not included in the per-100g composite values. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate for the solid food components only. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on steamed white rice as the dominant carbohydrate source per University of Sydney GI Database.
